spec_match.rationale: Composes `Iterator.concat(...)` with map / filter / take / drop helpers across six source types (array, Set, generator, Map.entries, string, infinite generator), a cleanup-instrumented generator source, and an eager-spread alternative - exactly the TC39 proposal's compositional story.

interactive.rationale: Live source picker (order matters), helper toggles, lazy-vs-eager strategy toggle, per-source pull counters, a cleanup diagnostics toggle, and an 'eager would hang' warning when an infinite source is included.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises finite/infinite/heterogeneous sources, chained helpers, lazy-vs-eager, per-source pull counts, and an eager-hang warning. The page also now calls out the sync-only stream boundary and shows generator cleanup.

edge_cases.rationale: Infinite-source-after-finite is the canonical test for lazy concat, and the page leads with that exact framing. Eager mode is correctly pre-checked to avoid hanging, multi-source ordering is exposed as a control, `.take(5)` cleanup is visible through a generator `finally` log, and the page states that `ReadableStream` and event feeds belong in async iterator or Streams APIs.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5193275975794688 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven, opt toggles use class-based on/off states, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add `flatMap` / `reduce` helpers
openQuestions.detail: The TC39 proposal includes the full iterator-helper suite (`flatMap`, `reduce`, `every`, `some`, `find`, `forEach`). The builder currently exposes only the four most common helpers. Add the remaining three or four so the page covers the full proposal surface.

summary: Strong interactive surface for lazy vs eager composition, per-source pull counters, infinite + finite composition, generator cleanup through `.return()`, and the sync-vs-async stream boundary. Verified in Chrome Canary 151 with chrome-devtools-mcp by running the lazy default, eager blocked state, cleanup toggle, and reset path. Remaining follow-up: expose more iterator helpers.
